summary: Migrated the large lb collision translation unit from raw asm into src/melee/lb/lbcollision.c, mostly by wrapping existing assembly in MWERKS_GEKKO inline asm while beginning selective C recovery. The PR explicitly scoped itself as a migration, not a full matching pass: the author wrote, "I started matching it but just migration is enough for this PR." It added lbcollision.c to obj_files.mk, moved/expanded hitbox-related declarations into lbcollision.h, introduced a shared HitVictim type, and cleaned up several duplicate or local extern declarations exposed by the migration.
what_changed: Added a new 5970-line src/melee/lb/lbcollision.c and removed nearly all contents of asm/melee/lb/lbcollision.s, moving the lbcollision code into the C source tree.
Added $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/lb/lbcollision.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk while the asm lbcollision object entry remains present in the surrounding build list.

Implemented func_80005BB0 in C using Hitbox fields sfx_kind and sfx_severity and the lbl_803B9880 sound-effect lookup table, calling func_80024184 with the special case for sfx_kind 0xD and severity 2.
Implemented func_8000ACFC in C as a HitVictim log membership check over hitbox->victims_1 using ARRAY_SIZE, returning true when the victim entity is present.

Preserved most migrated functions as inline asm blocks under #ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO with #pragma push/#pragma pop and clang-format off comments, including functions such as func_80005C44, func_80005EBC, func_80005FC0, func_80006094, func_800067F8, func_80006E58, func_800077A0, func_80007BCC, func_80007ECC, func_8000805C, func_80008248, func_80008440, func_80009F54, func_8000A044, and others.

Moved the item-specific itHitVictim layout into a shared lb-level HitVictim struct with fields HSD_GObj* entity and s32 iframes, then changed itHit.x74_tipLog and itHit.xD4_damageLog to use HitVictim.
Expanded struct Hitbox in lbcollision.h with more explicit offsets: x1C, a union exposing byte x43 and x43 bitfields, x44/x45, x4C, x58/x5C/x60/x64, and victims_1/victims_2 arrays at 0x74 and 0xD4.
Added HitVictim to src/melee/lb/forward.h and removed itHitVictim from src/melee/it/forward.h.
Added func_80024184 to lbaudio_ax.h and removed local extern declarations for lbcollision functions from fighter source files once header prototypes covered them.

Adjusted prototypes in lbcollision.h, including HSD_JObjUnkMtxPtr from void* to void, func_80008248 from s32 to bool with a bool first argument, func_8000ACFC from Fighter* to UNK_T, func_80007AFC from bool to void, and func_80008428 to take Hitbox*.
Removed duplicate declarations in GXDisplayList.h and tev.h.
Changed MSL string strncpy/strcpy declarations and definitions from __restrict to ATTRIBUTE_RESTRICT.
smart_moves: Kept the PR scope narrow: instead of forcing a full match of a very large collision module, it first made lbcollision build as a C translation unit with inline asm, creating a better base for incremental matching.
Recovered small, tractable routines in C during migration: func_80005BB0 exposes the sfx_kind/sfx_severity table access, and func_8000ACFC exposes the HitVictim victim-log scan.
Used MWERKS_GEKKO-gated inline asm and #pragma push/#pragma pop around migrated assembly, which preserves compiler-specific assembly without pretending the code is portable C.
Promoted HitVictim to the lb collision layer rather than keeping a duplicate item-local itHitVictim, making fighter/item collision logs share one type.
Converted opaque padding in Hitbox into named fields only where offsets were evidenced by the migrated code, while leaving unknown regions as padding instead of over-naming them.
Centralized prototypes in headers and removed stale local externs from fighter files, reducing drift between call sites and the newly migrated translation unit.
decomp_lessons: Large asm modules can be migrated before they are fully matched; preserving inline asm in a C file can unblock type/header work and make future matching smaller and more searchable.

When a migration reveals the same struct layout in multiple subsystems, prefer a shared forward-declared type in the owning library layer; here HitVictim belongs with lb collision, not item-only types.

Keep offset comments and padding in partially understood structs. The Hitbox changes show a useful pattern: introduce known fields at fixed offsets while retaining byte arrays for still-unknown spans.
Header cleanup is part of migration work: adding real prototypes often allows local externs and duplicate declarations to be removed elsewhere.
Inline asm migrations may require constants to be materialized as C globals with the same lbl_ names and exact f32/f64 types to preserve references from asm blocks.
Use compiler abstraction macros such as ATTRIBUTE_RESTRICT instead of raw __restrict when moving code across contexts that may be sensitive to compiler dialect.
naming_conventions: Preserved unknown function names in address form, e.g. func_80005BB0, func_80005C44, func_80007BCC, func_8000ACFC.
Preserved label-style data names such as lbl_803B9880, lbl_804D79F0, lbl_804D7A00, and lbl_804D7A10 where semantic names were not yet established.
Named the shared victim-log type HitVictim, with fields entity and iframes, replacing the item-local itHitVictim name.
Used Hitbox fields sfx_kind and sfx_severity in the newly C-coded sound-effect helper.
Kept unknown Hitbox members in offset form such as x1C, x43, x4C, x58, x5C, x60, x64, victims_1, and victims_2 rather than inventing semantics.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Migrated raw .s code into inline asm functions guarded by #ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO.
Wrapped inline asm regions with #pragma push/#pragma pop and nofralloc, preserving original prolog/epilog behavior where needed.
Used clang-format off comments around inline asm blocks to avoid formatting churn.
Lifted small leaf/utility routines to C first while leaving complex geometry, hitbox, hurtbox, GX drawing, and JObj-related routines in asm.
Declared C constants for asm-referenced floating-point labels, including 1e-5, -1e-5, 0, 1, 0.5, 2, 3, 4, M_PI, 5, and related lbl_804D7Axx symbols.
Retained address-label function names and labels inside inline asm, enabling later one-function-at-a-time matching.
review_feedback: One review by r-burns approved the PR; the review body was empty.
No issue comments or review comments were present in the provided slice.
The author explicitly noted that matching had started but the accepted scope was migration only: "I started matching it but just migration is enough for this PR."
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that match individual functions migrated in PR 723 from inline asm to C.
Search for later renames of HitVictim fields entity and iframes or Hitbox victims_1/victims_2.
Check whether HSD_JObjUnkMtxPtr was later moved out of lbcollision or given a more accurate prototype.
Search for later cleanup of lbl_803B9880 and func_80005BB0 sound-effect semantics.
Find follow-up changes replacing lbcollision.h placeholder/UNK_T usage with concrete fighter/item types.
